briefing.title: Market Leadership by Volume
briefing.detail: Purse Maison leads all stores in units sold MTD at 204 and sold value at ₱44.4M, nearly double second-place Miss Manila Luxe (₱27.6M) and 2× Shop with K (₱21.3M) — but Miss Manila Luxe's 579-unit sell-through at avg ₱47.7K signals a booming entry-luxury segment we are not capturing.
briefing.action: Evaluate adding 30–50 entry-luxury SKUs (Prada, Gucci, Saint Laurent sub-₱80K) to exploit the high-velocity segment Miss Manila Luxe dominates.

briefing.title: Sell-Through Velocity vs. Peers
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's avg days-to-sell is 16.7 days vs. market leaders Canone Bags Prime (9.0d) and Miss Manila Luxe (9.7d); at current pace, the ₱290.5M inventory turns in roughly 23 weeks — healthy but improvable, especially given zero aging-90 stock.
briefing.action: Identify the slowest-moving SKUs within the 1,670-unit inventory (particularly in 'Other' at 29.1%) and queue a 10–15% markdown sweep this week to accelerate turns.

briefing.title: Hermès Concentration Risk & Opportunity
briefing.detail: Hermès is 39.4% of Purse Maison's mix and dominates top-sold models (Birkin, Kelly, Constance); The Orange Box carries 50.7% Hermès at avg ticket ₱223.6K vs. our ₱173.9K, suggesting we may be pricing Hermès below its market ceiling.
briefing.action: Audit Hermès listings priced within 10% of market floor and test 5–8% price increases on Birkin and Kelly units currently not at the market-lowest ask.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo Overhang
briefing.detail: Purse Maison holds 25 units of Chanel Double Flap Jumbo — by far the largest single-model position — with our lowest ask at ₱188K vs. market low ₱170K across 41 market listings; this is the most crowded, price-competitive Chanel slot in the market.
briefing.action: Hold pricing on Jumbo units above ₱200K (differentiate on condition/hardware), and mark down the bottom 5 units to ₱172K–₱175K to clear inventory and free capital.

briefing.title: KC Luxury Bags & Luxe In — Zero Sales Anomaly
briefing.detail: KC Luxury Bags (154 units, ₱48.7M inventory, avg ticket ₱316K) and Luxe In (524 units, ₱23M) have zero sales MTD — KC's high-ticket Hermès-heavy mix may signal pricing stagnation or a new entrant still onboarding.
briefing.action: Monitor KC Luxury Bags weekly; if they begin discounting their ₱316K avg-ticket Hermès units, prepare a competitive response on overlapping Kelly 28 and Birkin 30 listings.

briefing.title: Louis Vuitton Underweight vs. Competitors
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's LV mix is only 6.2% vs. Miss Manila Luxe (9.1%), The Bag Hub (8.2%), and Canone Bags Prime (11.9%) — LV is a top-selling model at three competitors and drives high-velocity, lower-ticket volume.
briefing.action: Source 20–30 LV units (Neverfull, Speedy, Onthego) at sub-₱80K to broaden customer entry points and improve overall sell-through velocity.

alerts: Miss Manila Luxe sold 579 units MTD at ₱9.7-day velocity — nearly 3× Purse Maison's unit volume at 1/2.5 the avg ticket; the entry-luxury (<₱100K) segment is extremely active and underserved by Purse Maison.

Birkin 30: Purse Maison's lowest ask is ₱728K vs. market low ₱520K — we are ₱208K above market floor with only 4 units; verify condition premiums justify spread or risk stagnation as The Orange Box and The Bag Hub both move Birkins actively.
Kelly 28: Market has 27 listings with floor at ₱480K; Purse Maison holds 10 units at ₱735K floor — a ₱255K premium that requires strong condition/hardware justification given competition depth.

Mini Kelly (Kelly 20): Purse Maison's 2 units priced at ₱1.54M vs. market high of ₱1.68M — pricing is competitive but near ceiling; market has only 5 listings total, making this a thin, fast-moving opportunity to restock if sourcing allows.

Luxury Wish has only 168 units but ₱18M sold MTD with 14.1-day velocity and a top-5 Constance appearance — this small, focused competitor is punching well above its size and may be winning on curation and pricing precision.

pricing.suggestion: Hold current floor at ₱1.288M (only ₱8K above market low of ₱1.28M); market is thin at 14 listings and Purse Maison's 7 units benefit from depth premium — consider ₱1.30M–₱1.35M on Grade A units to capture margin before restocking.

Purse Maison floor of ₱728K is ₱208K above market low (₱520K) — reprice the lowest-condition unit to ₱580K–₱620K to stay competitive; retain higher-condition units at ₱728K+ with explicit grade/hardware callouts.

With market floor at ₱480K and 27 listings competing, reprice at least 2–3 of the 10 units to ₱520K–₱560K to drive velocity; maintain premium pricing (₱735K+) only on exotic or pristine-condition pieces with documented provenance.

Floor is ₱695K vs. market low ₱650K across 16 listings — a ₱45K gap that is defensible if condition is strong; hold pricing but ensure listings emphasize hardware color and condition grade to justify the premium, especially vs. Luxury Wish which actively sells Constance.

Purse Maison is tied at market floor (₱188K vs. ₱170K market low) across a 41-listing crowded market with 25 units in house — mark down the 5 weakest-condition units to ₱172K–₱178K immediately; price remaining units at ₱195K–₱220K with grade/color premiums to avoid a race-to-bottom.

Purse Maison matches market floor exactly at ₱158K with 13 units — this is a high-liquidity slot (31 market listings); hold ₱158K as entry price for C-grade units but push B/A-grade units to ₱175K–₱210K to lift avg ticket and protect margin.
